rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE ERNEST V. RHINE TRUST
rulings.Calendar Matter: Respondents Notice Of Motion And Motion To Strike Allegations In Petitioners Second Amended Trust Contest Petition
rulings.Rulings: Kimberly Rhine Gagosian's Motion to Strike Allegations in Petitioner's Second Amended Trust Contest Petition ("the SAP") is GRANTED in part and DENIED in part. Under CCP § 436, the Court has authority to strike out "any irrelevant, false, or improper matter inserted in any pleading." Accordingly, the Motion is granted with respect to the following: -p. 3, para. 11, line 27 through p. 4:1 -p. 5, para. 24:24-28; -p. 6, para. 31:24-26; -p. 7, para. 36:20; -p. 8, para. 40:15-17; -p. 9, para. 47 in its entirety; -p. 11, para. 58:19-21; -p. 11, paras. 59, 60 in their entirety ; -p. 12, para. 63 in its entirety; -p. 12, para. 66:22-24; -p. 12, para. 68: 28 ("¿the Decedent was caught in Kimberly's web of lies¿"); -p. 15, para. 80 in its entirety; -p. 16, para. 92:21-22 ("The presumption of undue influence has been met in this matter."); -p. 16, para. 93 in its entirety; -p. 17, para. 96 in its entirety; -p. 18, Conclusion: 1-2. The Motion is denied as to all other allegations Gagosian seeks to strike. Any party wishing to contest this tentative ruling must so notify the courtroom clerk (415-551-3702) and other parties no later than 4 p.m. on Wednesday, November 7, 2018.

rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE ERNEST V. RHINE TRUST
rulings.Calendar Matter: Respondent Kim Gagosians Notice Of Demurrer And Demurrer To Second Amended Trust Contest Petition
rulings.Rulings: Kimberly Rhine Gagosian's Demurrer to Petitioner's Second Amended Trust Contest Petition is OVERRULED on all grounds. Lydia has standing to pursue her undue influence claim as the Decedent's surviving spouse and an "interested person" (Probate Code § 48(a)) with a pecuniary interest in the devolution of the trust estate. The Court finds that the case of Barefoot v. Jennings is inapplicable as Lydia has not brought her petition pursuant to Probate Code § 17200, and the Court declines to construe her petition as being brought under that section. (See Lintz v. Lintz (2014) 222 Cal.App.4th 1346, 1358.) Gagosian has also specially demurred to allegations regarding "public policy" and due execution (i.e., invalidity of two required signatures), which is inappropriate. Neither a general nor a special demurrer can be directed to anything except the entire complaint or a count; it will not lie to part of a cause of action or count. (Campbell v. Genshlea (1919) 180 Cal. 213, 217.) Those are mere allegations in the SAP and thus cannot be disposed of by way of demurrer. Further, the Court has already struck paragraph 47 (regarding violation of public policy) from the SAP. Further, Lydia has sufficiently alleged all of the elements of a cause of action for undue influence. With respect to Petitioner's "undue benefit" allegations, the Court finds that Petitioner has stated enough facts for a future trier of fact to determine that the benefit that inured to Respondent Kimberly Gagosian as a result of the contested trust amendments was "undue" in light of e.g., the respective relationships of the parties. (See Estate of Sarabia (1990) 221 Cal.App.3d 599, 607.) Additionally, the Court strikes the following from Emilie Calhoun's Declaration in Support of Demurrer per CCP § 436: -p. 2, para. 3 in its entirety; -pp. 3-4, paras. 5-7 in their entirety; -pp. 4-5, paras. 8-12. The Court also strikes the following from Kimberly Gagosian's Memorandum of Points and Authorities in Support of Demurrer: -p.7:6-23 through p. 8:1-7. Any party wishing to contest this tentative ruling must so notify the courtroom clerk (415-551-3702) and other parties no later than 4 p.m. on Wednesday, November 7, 2018.
